'''Elliot Wallis''' (born 10 May 2000) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er or {{rlp|ce}} for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He has previously played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]], and the [[Coventry Bears]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]]. He has spent time on loan from Hull KR at the [[York City Knights]] and the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[2020 RFL Championship|Championship]], and on dual registration from Castleford at the [[Midlands Hurricanes]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]].

==Background==
Wallis was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], [[East Riding of Yorkshire]], England. He is of [[Nigerians|Nigerian]] heritage.<ref>{{cite web|date=4 September 2019|title=Meet Elliot Wallis - The explosive Hull KR winger called up by new Nigerian national team|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/meet-elliot-wallis-explosive-hull-19437350|publisher=Mirror|accessdate=1 November 2019}}</ref>

Wallis played junior rugby league for [[Skirlaugh A.R.L.F.C.|Skirlaugh Bulls]]. Wallis progressed through the ranks at the City of Hull Academy before he started spending more time with the [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]' first-team squad.<ref>{{cite web |date=8 October 2018 |title=Wallis' player profile |url=https://www.hullkr.co.uk/player/29085/}}</ref>

==Playing career==
=== Hull Kingston Rovers ===
On 13 May 2018, Wallis made his [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] début against the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Challenge Cup]].<ref>{{cite web |date=13 May 2018 |title=Challenge Cup: Hull KR 10-28 Wigan Warriors |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/44070574 |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> His first [[Super League]] appearance came 2 weeks later on 25 May 2018, also against Wigan.<ref>{{Cite web |date=25 May 2018 |title=Super League: Hull KR 24-8 Wigan Warriors |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/44212348 |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In June, he signed his first professional contract with Hull KR, agreeing to a three-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=13 June 2018 |title=Elliot Wallis commits long-term future to Hull KR |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/elliot-wallis-commits-long-term-future-to-hull-kr |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Westmorland |first=Gareth |date=12 June 2018 |title=Elliot Wallis reveals shock after signing long-term Hull KR deal |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-news/elliot-wallis-reveals-shock-after-1668278 |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=Hull Daily Mail}}</ref> On 20 July, Wallis scored his first tries for Hull KR against the [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{cite web |date=21 July 2018 |title=Hull KR 20-34 Warrington: Tyrone Roberts stars as Wolves go third |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/hull-k-r-vs-warrington/48399 |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

In April 2021, shortly following a recall from his loan move to [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]], Wallis departed Hull KR by mutual consent.<ref>{{Cite web |date=17 April 2021 |title=Elliot Wallis departs Rovers |url=https://hullkr.co.uk/elliot-wallis-departs-rovers/ |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=Hull Kingston Rovers}}</ref>

==== York City Knights (loan) ====
Wallis joined Championship side [[York City Knights]] on loan for the 2020 season.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=13 November 2019 |title=York sign Hull KR winger on loan |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/york-sign-hull-kr-winger-on-loan |access-date=9 July 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Kilbride |first=Jacob |date=12 November 2019 |title=York City Knights sign Hull KR winger Elliot Wallis on loan |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/18031992.york-city-knights-sign-hull-kr-winger-elliot-wallis-loan/ |access-date=9 July 2023 |website=The York Press}}</ref> He made 3 appearances for the club across February and March before the season was [[Impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on rugby league#Championship and League 1|suspended and later abandoned]] as a result of the [[COVID-19 pandemic in the United Kingdom|COVID-19 pandemic]].<ref name="RLRKC" />

==== Bradford Bulls (loan) ====
On 11 November 2020, Wallis signed a one-year loan deal with the [[Bradford Bulls]] for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=11 Nov 2020|accessdate=3 Jan 2021|title=Bradford sign winger Elliot Wallis on loan from Hull KR|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/bradford-sign-winger-elliot-wallis-on-loan-from-hull-kr/}}</ref> He was assigned squad number 21.<ref>{{Cite web |date=12 February 2021 |title=Bulls reveal 2021 squad numbers |url=https://bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/860/bulls-reveal-2021-squad-numbers |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=Bradford Bulls}}</ref> He featured in the Bulls' pre-season friendlies against [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] and [[Keighley Cougars|Keighley]], but departed the club on 15 April 2021 without making a competitive appearance.<ref>{{Cite web |date=15 April 2021 |title=Wallis returns to Rovers |url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/932/wallis-returns-to-rovers |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=Bradford Bulls}}</ref>

=== Coventry Bears ===
In 2021, following his exit from [[Hull KR]], Wallis signed for the [[Coventry Bears]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]].<ref name="TRL Cas Deal">{{Cite web |last=Ibbetson |first=Stephen |date=27 November 2022 |title=Reserves system a saviour for newest Castleford Tigers player Elliot Wallis |url=https://www.totalrl.com/reserves-system-a-saviour-for-newest-castleford-tigers-player-elliot-wallis/ |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=Total RL}}</ref> He made his Coventry debut on 27 June against [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]],<ref>{{Cite web |date=27 June 2021 |title=Coventry Bears 16 - 34 Doncaster |url=https://stats.rugbyleaguerecords.com/matchdetails.php?tselect=13132 |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=RL Record Keepers' Club}}</ref> and scored his first try for the club against [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]] on 4 July.<ref>{{Cite web |date=4 July 2021 |title=Coventry Bears 46 - 44 Hunslet |url=https://stats.rugbyleaguerecords.com/matchdetails.php?tselect=13147 |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=RL Record Keepers' Club}}</ref> While away from full-time rugby league, Wallis took up [[boxing]] full-time and worked an [[Apprenticeships in the United Kingdom|apprenticeship]] in leadership and management.<ref name="YP Cas Deal">{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=6 February 2023 |title=Former Hull KR youngster Elliot Wallis ready to make most of Castleford Tigers lifeline |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/former-hull-kr-youngster-elliot-wallis-ready-to-make-most-of-castleford-tigers-lifeline-4015591 |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref>

=== Castleford Tigers ===
In 2022, Wallis joined the [[Castleford Tigers]] on trial, to play for the reserves side.<ref name="TRL Cas Deal" /><ref name="YP Cas Deal" /> At the end of the season, Castleford head coach [[Lee Radford]] said, "For me, he was head and shoulders one of our best reserve team players in 2022," and praised his attitude and physicality.<ref name="WE Cas Deal">{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=17 November 2022 |title=Elliot Wallis eager to take his second chance at Super League with Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/elliot-wallis-eager-to-take-his-second-chance-at-super-league-with-castleford-tigers-3922126 |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref> In November, the Tigers announced that Wallis would be promoted to the first team in 2023 after signing a one-year professional contract with the option of a further year.<ref>{{Cite web |date=16 November 2022 |title=Wallis moves up to first team |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8367 |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref name="WE Cas Deal" />

Wallis was assigned squad number 26 for the 2023 season.<ref>{{Cite web |date=20 January 2023 |title=Tigers announce squad numbers for 2023 |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8438 |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> He made his Castleford debut on 5 May against the [[Leigh Leopards]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=5 May 2023 |title=Super League: Leigh Leopards 30-6 Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65468145 |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He scored his first try for the Tigers at the [[Magic Weekend#2023: Newcastle|2023 Magic Weekend]] against the [[Leeds Rhinos]], showing "pace and precision to keep himself inside the chalk," by beating two defenders down the touchline.<ref>{{Cite web |date=3 June 2023 |title=Super League: Leeds Rhinos 24-26 Castleford - Tigers end six-game losing run |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65754053 |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Bazeley |first=Marc |date=3 June 2023 |title=Leeds Rhinos 24-26 Castleford Tigers: Jason Qareqare's converted try snatches vital Magic Weekend win |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/leeds-vs-castleford/58333 |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref> He missed three games due to a [[Pectoralis major|pectoral]] injury in June,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=8 June 2023 |title=Andy Last on why Elliot Wallis and Bureta Faraimo are absent and chances of Castleford Tigers debutant |url=https://www.totalrl.com/andy-last-on-why-elliot-wallis-and-bureta-faraimo-are-absent-and-chances-of-castleford-tigers-debutant/ |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=Total RL}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=13 June 2023 |title=Tigers player and injury update |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8635 |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers |archive-date=26 June 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230626223249/https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8635 |url-status=dead }}</ref> before scoring a brace against [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] on his return.<ref>{{Cite web |date=7 July 2023 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 16-34 Leigh: Leopards maintain pressure on leaders Catalans |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66119096 |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> The mid-season departure of fellow winger [[Bureta Faraimo]] afforded Wallis a consistent place in the starting line-up, featuring in every game throughout July and August.<ref name="RLRKC" />

==== Midlands Hurricanes (dual registration) ====
Having previously represented the club under its former name Coventry, Wallis featured for the [[Midlands Hurricanes]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]] during the 2022 season through their dual registration agreement with Castleford. He made 4 appearances and scored 3 tries.<ref name="RLRKC" />

Wallis returned to the Hurricanes at the beginning of the 2023 season. Following a red card against [[Cornwall R.L.F.C.|Cornwall]], he was referred to tribunal with a striking charge, and subsequently received a five-match ban.<ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=23 February 2023 |title=Castleford winger referred to tribunal over striking charge |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-winger-referred-to-tribunal-over-striking-charge |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=1 March 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers winger banned for five games following striking charge whilst on dual-registration |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-winger-banned-for-five-games-following-striking-charge-whilst-on-dual-registration/ |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=Total RL}}</ref> In total, he made 3 appearances and scored a further 3 tries for the club.<ref name="RLRKC" />

===Huddersfield Giants===
On 11 Oct 2023 it was announced that he had joined the [[Huddersfield Giants]] on a 4-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=11 Oct 2023|title=Castleford Tigers and Huddersfield Giants agree swap deal for wing pair|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-huddersfield-giants-agree-swap-deal-for-wing-pair-elliot-wallis-innes-senior}}</ref> He scored on his Super League debut in a 16-8 win at [[Leigh Leopards]].<ref>https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/super-league-2024/round-1/leigh-leopards-vs-huddersfield-giants/summary.html</ref>

On 5 November 2025 it was reported that he had left [[Huddersfield Giants]] by mutual agreement<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=5 Nov 2025|author=Louis Chapman Coombe|title=Super League winger makes immediate exit to pursue ‘new challenge’|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/super-league-winger-makes-immediate-exit-to-pursue-new-challenge}}</ref>

====Leeds Rhinos (loan)====
On 1 May 2025 it was reported that he had signed for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]] on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=1 May 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=Huddersfield Giants winger Elliot Wallis joins Super League rivals on loan|url=https://www.totalrl.com/huddersfield-giants-winger-elliot-wallis-joins-super-league-rivals-on-loan/}}</ref>

====Castleford Tigers (loan)====
On 13 August 2025 it was reported that he had signed for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]] on loan for the remainder of the 2025 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=13 Aug 2025|author=Louis Chapman Coombe|title=Huddersfield Giants star makes cross-Super League switch after Leeds Rhinos loan|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/huddersfield-giants-star-makes-cross-super-league-switch-after-leeds-rhinos-loan}}</ref>

===London Broncos===
On 13 November 2025 it was reported that he had signed for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=13 Nov 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Elliot Wallis signs for London Broncos following Huddersfield Giants exit|url=https://www.totalrl.com/elliot-wallis-signs-for-london-broncos-following-huddersfield-giants-exit/}}</ref>
[[File:Elliot Wallis.jpg|thumb|right|Wallis warming up with the London Broncos in 2026]]
